dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 3959 RH: Protecting Private Job Creators Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Downing (for himself and Mr. Fields) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services
Additional sponsors: Mr. Gottheimer, Mr. David Scott of Georgia, Mrs. Wagner, Mr. Sessions, Mr. Moore of North Carolina, and Mr. Lawler
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on June 12, 2025

form.official-title: To except quotations of fixed-income securities from certain regulatory requirements, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Private Job Creators Act.

section.header: Exception relating to quotations of fixed-income securities

subsection.text: Section 240.15c2–11 of title 17, Code of Federal Regulations, shall not apply with respect to quotations of fixed-income securities.
In this section, the term fixed-income security means—

paragraph.text: any note, bond, debenture, certificate of deposit for a security, certificate of deposit, asset-backed security, or any other evidence of indebtedness; and

any security described under paragraph (1) that is convertible, with or without consideration, into any equity security or carrying any warrant or right to subscribe to or purchase any equity security.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
